title: Interim Clinical Considerations for Use of Vaccine for Monkeypox Prevention in the United States | Monkeypox | CDC
text:

Vaccine doses should not be administered before the minimum interval (i.e., 28 days). Nevertheless, if the second dose is inadvertently administered before the minimum interval, the dose may not need to be repeated. Please refer to " Table 7. Vaccine Administration Errors and Deviations. "
Administration 
Subcutaneous (Subcut) administration 
Subcutaneous administration involves injecting the vaccine into the fatty tissue, typically over the triceps in people aged 12 months and older, or in the anterolateral thigh for people younger than age 12 months. CDC offers a short training video about subcutaneous vaccine administration and job aid [PDF – 1 page, 200KB] .
Intradermal (ID) administration 
Learn about intradermal administration of JYNNEOS 
Interchangeability of dosing regimens 
In eligible individuals, the dosing regimens are interchangeable. For example, a person aged 18 years or older who received one JYNNEOS vaccine dose intradermally may receive a second dose subcutaneously at the recommended interval (i.e., 28 days) to complete the vaccination series.
